name: Vienna
localTips: Buy a weekly or 24/48/72-hour Wiener Linien pass — trams, buses and the U-Bahn run on an honour system with no gates, but fines for riding without a validated ticket are steep.
A Viennese coffee house is a place to linger for hours over one coffee with a free glass of water — nobody will rush you, and rushing yourself misses the point.
Standing-room tickets for the State Opera and other top houses go on sale about 80 minutes before curtain and cost only a few euros — queue at the side entrance.
Many state museums (including parts of the Kunsthistorisches Museum's atrium) are free, and the Museumsquartier's outer courtyards are free public space day and night.
Jaywalking is taken seriously in Vienna — wait for the green Ampelmännchen even with an empty street, especially if children or police are nearby.

activities.title: Stephansplatz & St. Stephen's Cathedral exterior
activities.description: Vienna's Gothic centrepiece, with its famous tiled roof and soaring south tower, anchors the old city — the square around it is Vienna's beating heart at any hour.

activities.title: Ringstrasse grand boulevard walk
activities.description: Circle the monumental 19th-century boulevard past the Opera, Parliament, City Hall and Burgtheater — Vienna's imperial architecture on continuous display, free to admire.

activities.title: Naschmarkt browsing
activities.description: Vienna's biggest and oldest market, over half a kilometre of produce stalls, spice shops and food stands, ending in a Saturday flea market at the western end.

activities.title: Prater park & Wurstelprater
activities.description: A vast public park with the historic Riesenrad ferris wheel and funfair at one end, and miles of chestnut-lined avenues and meadows the rest of the way.

activities.title: Belvedere Palace gardens
activities.description: The formal baroque gardens between the Upper and Lower Belvedere palaces are free to wander, with fountains, sphinx statues and a skyline view back over the city.

activities.title: MuseumsQuartier courtyards
activities.description: One of the world's largest cultural districts has free public courtyards filled with the city's famous modular 'Enzi' loungers — a social hub day and night, no ticket needed.

activities.title: Donauinsel (Danube Island)
activities.description: A 21-km artificial island built as a flood barrier, now Vienna's summer playground for swimming, biking and riverside beer gardens along the Neue Donau.

activities.title: Hundertwasserhaus exterior
activities.description: A residential building with uneven floors, rooftop trees and no straight lines by design — Friedensreich Hundertwasser's rebellion against conventional architecture.

activities.title: Karlsplatz & Karlskirche
activities.description: A domed baroque church fronted by a reflecting pool and flanked by two enormous spiral columns — one of Vienna's most photogenic squares, free to enjoy from outside.

activities.title: Albertina modern art passage (free zone)
activities.description: The Albertina's underground passage next to the museum showcases free rotating urban art installations lit along its walls — a quick cultural stop needing no ticket.

activities.title: Votivkirche & Sigmund Freud Park
activities.description: A twin-spired neo-Gothic church facing a quiet university-district park — far fewer tourists than Stephansdom but just as striking against the sky.

activities.title: Rathausplatz Christmas market
activities.description: Vienna's biggest Christmas market, set against the neo-Gothic City Hall lit up and framed by an ice-skating trail winding through the park.

activities.title: Stadtpark & the golden Strauss statue
activities.description: A landscaped English-style park with the famous gilded statue of Johann Strauss II mid-violin — a favourite lunch-break escape for office workers.

activities.title: Am Hof & the old town backstreets
activities.description: Wander the quiet baroque squares of Am Hof and Freyung, tucked just behind the main tourist drag, with fewer crowds and just as much architectural detail.

activities.title: Augarten baroque park
activities.description: A quiet formal park in Leopoldstadt with tree-lined avenues, home to the Vienna Boys' Choir and the historic Augarten porcelain manufactory — far from the tourist trail.

activities.title: Coffee house culture at Café Central
activities.description: A vaulted, marble-columned coffee house once frequented by Trotsky and Freud — order a Melange and read the papers on wooden stands like everyone else.
activities.insiderNote: Skip the queue at the ornate entrance by visiting right at opening (7:30am weekdays) — same grandeur, none of the wait.

activities.title: Heuriger wine tavern in Grinzing
activities.description: Vienna's own wine-growing suburb has dozens of family-run heuriger taverns serving this year's young wine with buffet-style Austrian food in leafy courtyards.
activities.insiderNote: Look for a green wreath or branch (Buschen) hung over the door — it signals the tavern is currently pouring its own new wine.

activities.title: Brunnenmarkt in Ottakring
activities.description: Vienna's longest street market, a multicultural strip of Turkish, Balkan and Middle Eastern stalls where locals actually do their weekly shop.
activities.insiderNote: Saturday's flea-market extension is where locals hunt for bargains — arrive by 9am before the good stalls sell out.

activities.title: Cocktails at Loos American Bar
activities.description: A tiny 1908 Adolf Loos-designed bar the size of a living room, all dark marble and mirrors — one of the first modernist interiors in the world, still pouring drinks.
activities.insiderNote: It seats barely 20 people — arrive right at 6pm opening on a weeknight if you want a seat rather than standing by the door.

activities.title: Standing-room tickets at the State Opera
activities.description: World-class opera for the price of a coffee — the Staatsoper sells a few hundred standing-room spots for every performance, right beneath the chandeliers.
activities.insiderNote: Tickets go on sale roughly 80 minutes before curtain at the Stehplatz box office; bring a scarf to tie to the railing and reserve your spot, an old Viennese custom.

activities.title: Spittelberg cobbled lanes
activities.description: A pocket of Biedermeier-era houses turned artisan boutiques and courtyard restaurants, tucked behind the MuseumsQuartier — a quiet contrast to the grand Ring.
activities.insiderNote: The Christmas market here in December is smaller and far more atmospheric than Rathausplatz's — mulled wine among the cobblestones, not crowds.

activities.title: Sewers & underground of Vienna
activities.description: The sewer tunnels made famous in the film 'The Third Man' are open on a small guided walk beneath the city centre — cool, dripping, and genuinely atmospheric.
activities.insiderNote: Tours are seasonal and in German or English on alternating days — check the schedule at the Karlsplatz Third Man Museum before turning up.

activities.title: Late-night Würstelstand sausage stand
activities.description: Vienna's classic street-corner sausage stands stay open long after the bars close — Bitzinger by the Albertina is the most famous, serving Käsekrainer to the after-party crowd.
activities.insiderNote: Order a Käsekrainer 'eh' (with everything) and a small beer — it's a Viennese rite of passage after a night out.

activities.title: Schönbrunn Palace tour
activities.description: The Habsburgs' summer palace, with gilded state rooms, Maria Theresa's apartments and formal gardens climbing to the Gloriette viewpoint above.

activities.title: Kunsthistorisches Museum entry
activities.description: Bruegels, Vermeers and one of the world's great old master collections, housed in a palatial building with a dome fresco worth the ticket alone.

activities.title: Belvedere Palace: Klimt's 'The Kiss'
activities.description: The Upper Belvedere holds the world's largest Klimt collection, including 'The Kiss', inside a gold-and-white baroque palace built for Prince Eugene.

activities.title: Giant Riesenrad ferris wheel ride
activities.description: The 1897 wheel made famous in 'The Third Man' still turns slowly over the Prater, its wooden cabins giving a gentle panoramic view over the whole city.
activities.bookingQuery: Riesenrad giant ferris wheel ticket Vienna

activities.title: Evening classical concert in period costume
activities.description: Orchestras in powdered wigs and period dress perform Mozart and Strauss favourites in gilded baroque halls — a tourist-friendly but genuinely enjoyable introduction to Viennese music.
activities.bookingQuery: Mozart Strauss concert period costume Vienna

activities.title: Sachertorte tasting at Hotel Sacher
activities.description: The original chocolate cake, invented here in 1832 — a slice with unsweetened whipped cream in the red-velvet Café Sacher is a Vienna rite of passage.

activities.title: Wachau Valley day trip
activities.description: A day among the vineyard-terraced hills and riverside villages of the Danube's most scenic stretch, with a cruise past the ruined Dürnstein castle.
activities.bookingQuery: Wachau Valley Danube day trip from Vienna

activities.title: Spanish Riding School morning training
activities.description: Watch the famous white Lipizzaner stallions train in the imperial Winter Riding School's chandeliered hall — a centuries-old tradition still performed daily.
activities.bookingQuery: Spanish Riding School morning training ticket Vienna

activities.title: Donauturm revolving restaurant
activities.description: Vienna's tallest structure has an observation deck and a slowly revolving restaurant 170 metres up, with views stretching to the Alps on a clear day.
activities.bookingQuery: Donauturm tower observation deck ticket Vienna

activities.title: Vienna Woods & wine cellar night tour
activities.description: An evening trip into the hills outside the city to a family-run wine cellar for a tasting and hearty dinner, with the vineyards and forest quiet after dark.
activities.bookingQuery: Vienna Woods wine cellar dinner tour
